sections.title: 2023 Amendments
sections.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty -sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.
sections.retrieval_text:
2023 Amendments
number=2023
level=1
Following decisions taken at the Forty -sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.

page_spans.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty -sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to dairy permeate powders, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard, intended for further processing and/or as ingredient in other foods.

reference.document: CXS 331-2017 - Standard for Dairy Permeate Powders

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Dairy permeate powders are dried milk products characterized by a high content of lactose: Whey permeate powder is the dairy permeate powder manufactured from whey permeate. Whey permeate is obtained by removing whey protein, but not lactose, from whey. Milk permeate powder is the dairy permeate powder manufactured from milk permeate.

sections.title: Composition
sections.text: Criteria Dairy permeate powder Whey permeate powder Milk permeate powder Minimum lactose, anhydrous (a) (m/m) 76.0% 76.0% 76.0% Maximum nitrogen (m/m) 1.1% 1.1% 0.8 % Maximum milk fat (m/m) 1.5% 1.5% 1.5% Maximum ash (m/m) 14.0% 12.0% 12.0% Maximum moisture (b) (m/m) 5.0% 5.0% 5.0% (a) Although the products may contain both anhydrous lactose and lactose monohydrate, the lactose content is expressed as anhydrous lactose. 100 parts of lactose monohydrate contain 95 parts of anhydrous lactose. (b) The moisture content does not include the water of crystallization of the lactose. In accordance with the provision of Section 4.3.3 of the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the dairy permeate powders covered by this standard may be modified in composition to meet the desired end-product composition, for instance, partial demineralization. However, compositional modifications beyond the minima or maxima specified above for lactose, nitrogen, milk fat, ash and moisture are not considered to be in compliance with the Section 4.3.3 of the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999).

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).4 The milk used in the manufacture of the raw materials covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)4 and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug  residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),5 the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004)6 and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with any microbiological  criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).7

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name of the food shall be dairy permeate powder. Products complying with the relevant descriptions in Section 2 and compositions in Section 3.3 may be named milk permeate powder and whey permeate powder, respectively. Where appropriate in the country of sale, the name may be supplemented by the designation “lactose -rich deproteinized ____ powder”, the blank being filled with the term dairy, whey or milk, as appropriate to the nature of the product.

classification.pattern: Prepositional phrase indicating event + passive construction reporting an action on a regulatory section

components.event: decisions taken at the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022

classification.justification: The sentence reports an historical administrative action rather than expressing normative modality such as obligation, permission, or prohibition. It uses a passive construction following a temporal prepositional phrase, a pattern not in the known templates catalog, hence classified as a new pattern with undefined modality.
notes: Sentence serves as an informative statement rather than regulatory instruction.
No normative modality or condition is present to assign standard categories.
This kind of record or announcement sentence is outside typical regulatory normative templates.

components.Rationale: in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA

classification.justification: The sentence is a declarative informative statement reporting an administrative action and alignment process. It does not express modality such as obligation, permission, prohibition, condition, or limits. notes: Sentence is primarily informative and declarative, not normative; no known templates in catalog to match.

classification.pattern: [Subject] applies to [Object], in conformity with [Reference], intended for [Purpose].

components.subject: This standard
components.reference: description in Section 2 of this standard
components.purpose: intended for further processing and/or as ingredient in other foods

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by stating the scope of the standard's application, defining which products it covers and under what description, thus serving as a normative scope definition. notes: The sentence blends obligation with scope definition, which can overlap with condition or permission in other contexts, but the presence of 'applies to' and normativity indicates obligation.

classification.justification: The sentence issues a prescriptive action (imperative form) indicating an obligation without explicit modal verbs such as shall or must. The structure is a verb phrase followed by a prepositional phrase specifying the context. No strong match with existing templates exists, so a new pattern capturing imperative prescriptive action plus context is proposed.

sentences.original: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).4 The milk used in the manufacture of the raw materials covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)4 and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug  residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

classification.sentence: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).4 The milk used in the manufacture of the raw materials covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)4 and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug  residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
classification.function: Mandate compliance with specified maximum contaminant levels
classification.pattern: Subject (products or milk) + shall comply with + specified maximum levels + references to authoritative standards

components.subject_1: The products covered by this standard
components.criterion_1: maximum levels for contaminants specified in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
components.subject_2: The milk used in the manufacture of the raw materials covered by this standard
components.criterion_2: maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
components.criterion_3: maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation to comply with specified contaminant and residue limits for products and milk, referencing established standards. No exact template applies, but the structure reflects a mandatory compliance with quantitative limits, placing it in 'Limits and Targets' with an 'Obligation' modality. The structure involves multiple subjects and associated compliance criteria referencing authoritative standards. Template match score is low as no existing template exactly captures this extended compound sentence.
notes: Complex sentence with two related obligations for different subjects (products and milk) may challenge simpler template matching.

components.methods_specified: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999)10
components.relevance_clause: relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation mandating specific methods for compliance checking. It contains a purpose clause followed by a subject (methods) qualified by reference documents and provisions, ending in an obligation modal verb plus main verb. While no exact template exists, the structure fits typical obligation prescriptions with an introductory purpose phrase, suggesting an adapted template fit with moderate confidence.

sentences.original: The name of the food shall be dairy permeate powder.

classification.sentence: Where appropriate in the country of sale, the name may be supplemented by the designation 7actose -rich deproteinized ____ powder8, the blank being filled with the term dairy, whey or milk, as appropriate to the nature of the product.
classification.pattern: [Conditional qualifier at context level], [Action allowed] with [Specifying detail for filling blank] as appropriate to the product nature

components.conditional_qualifier: Where appropriate in the country of sale
components.allowed_action: the name may be supplemented by the designation “lactose -rich deproteinized ____ powder”
components.filling_instruction: the blank being filled with the term dairy, whey or milk
components.filling_condition: as appropriate to the nature of the product

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission for name supplementation under a contextual condition and specifies exact terms to fill a placeholder based on product nature. No known template fully represents this structure combining geographic appropriateness, permission, and specified filler instructions, leading to a new structural pattern.
notes: The sentence intertwines conditional context with permission and detailed product-specific instructions, which is unusual in typical regulatory templates.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR DAIRY PERMEATE POWDERS
pages.summary: Página contém título do documento 'STANDARD FOR DAIRY PERMEATE POWDERS' e cabeçalho 'CXS 331-2017'. Não há seções ou texto interno estruturado nesta página.

Página contendo duas seções principais numeradas como '2022 Amendments' e '2023 Amendments', cada uma seguida por seu texto explicativo. Cabeçalho 'CXS 331-2017 2' identificado. Não há continuidade entre páginas.

Página contém 4 seções principais (1 a 4), sendo a seção 3 com três subseções (3.1, 3.2, 3.3). A seção 3 continua na página seguinte, identificado por continues_on_next_page true. Texto organizado preserva hierarquia e associação correta do conteúdo.

Página 4 contém seções principais 4.2 a 8, com a seção 7 tendo duas subseções 7.1 e 7.2. A seção 4.2 continua da página anterior, indicada por is_continuation_from_previous_page true. Texto limpo e organizado para uso em RAG, sem inclusão do rodapé e cabeçalho repetitivo.

Página contém referências bibliográficas listadas sem títulos estruturais. Nenhum nó hierárquico identificado, pois o conteúdo é uma lista de referências. Conteúdo adequado para uso em RAG como texto simples, sem divisão estrutural adicional.
